rationale: Major friction across all axes: the dim blue-lit, dark-palette UGC of a young man dancing clashes hard with Marble Sort!'s pastel lavender, calm/satisfying brand world, and shows zero gameplay so it can't communicate the physics-tap sorting loop that drives installs in this genre. The implied audience (young male, late-night gaming energy, "after school") is a poor match for the 25-55 female casual puzzle player. Only consider testing if reshot with a female creator, daylight setting, pastel wardrobe, and a clear over-the-shoulder gameplay capture cutaway — otherwise skip.
